race.name: Cheaha Challenge Gran Fondo
race.tagline: 126 miles of Alabama savagery with 13,000ft that crushes souls twice over Cheaha.

vitals.field_size: Hundreds from 26+ states and 9+ countries, all levels chasing UCI glory
vitals.registration: Includes pre-ride party meal, free breakfast for riders/families, post-ride feast, finisher swag, BikeExpo, and packet pickup
vitals.prize_purse: UCI medals/jerseys for century winners, KOM/QOM awards, age group prizes
vitals.aid_stations: 11 rest stops stocked across all routes
vitals.cutoff_time: Strict: 12pm century to 84mi, 1pm Ultra extension or get rerouted
distance_options: Ultra: 126mi, 13,000ft — extreme loop with Chinnabee Lake bonus
UCI Century: 100mi, 9,714ft — double Cheaha summit brutality

climate.primary: Mild Southern spring — blue skies, clean mountain air
climate.description: May delivers ideal temps in the 60-80F range amid Talladega National Forest's four mountain zones. Clean air and blue skies make for perfect suffering conditions. Rare rain but humidity can sneak up on descents.

terrain.primary: Brutal paved mountain loops through Alabama's high country
terrain.surface: Smooth paved roads wind through Talladega National Forest with gaps, steep pitches, and ripping descents. Rails-to-trail options for cruises. State park construction reroutes ultras over Cheaha summit.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: UCI qualifier status and 30-year legacy punch it into T1 despite modest altitude. Elevation and adventure crush the math. Southern hospitality elevates the full package to elite sufferfest tier.

logistics.explanation: JSU campus start/finish is pedaler's paradise with expo and meals galore. Chief Ladiga ITT kicks off the party right. Cutoffs are brutal but fair—plan your suffering wisely. Family-friendly vibes make it a weekend win.

length.explanation: 126-mile Ultra is a monster that devours centuries like appetizers. Back-forth loop ensures no escape from the pain. Perfect for masochists eyeing GFNS glory. Anything shorter feels like a cop-out.

technicality.explanation: Paved perfection with gap descents and punchy kickers named to mock you. Double summits demand bike-handling chops. Rails-trail tame for newbs, but real course tests descending nerve. High stakes, high thrills.

elevation.explanation: 13,000ft over 126mi is soul-destroying gold. Century's 9,714ft already legendary—Ultra piles on Chinnabee cruelty. Cheaha twice? Pure Alabama vengeance. Vert junkies, this is your church.

climate.explanation: May's mild magic: 60-80F, blue skies, forest freshness. Humidity lurks to sap your will on climbs. Ideal for PRs if you dodge pollen. Southern spring at its deceptive best.

altitude.explanation: 2,407ft peak is hill country, not hypoxia. No thin-air excuses here. Low summit suits power pedaling over pure altitude grind. Cheaha feels huge locally, tame globally.

adventure.explanation: Talladega wilds, state park vistas, named climbs that taunt—pure exploratory brutality. Ultra's lake detour seals the epic. Forest immersion beats sterile centuries. Adventure on asphalt.

prestige.explanation: UCI/GFNS qualifier with world-champ shots—serious cred. 30-year king of Southern fondos. Modest field depth caps it, but badges shine. Legit badge for the palmares.

race_quality.explanation: NEABA nails execution: stocked stops, timing segments, swag even for DNF 'ashes'. Post-ride feasts fuel stories. Multiple distances welcome all. Gold-standard organization.

experience.explanation: Pre-party meals, expo buzz, family cruise options—total immersion. Finisher medals or hilarious ashes? Genius. Storytelling banquet cements memories. Unforgettable Southern spectacle.

community.explanation: Jacksonville's square charm and JSU hospitality ooze welcome. Riders from everywhere bond over shared pain. BBQ and festivals amplify the vibe. Tight-knit without cliques.

field_depth.explanation: Diverse pack from newbs to UCI hunters, but no pro peloton depth. Competitive enough for qualifiers. International flavor punches above regional weight. Solid, not stacked.

value.explanation: Meals, expo, swag, qualifiers for the price? Steal. Family perks seal it. No nickel-and-diming here. Biggest bang in Southern centuries.

expenses.explanation: Affordable entry, campus base keeps costs low. Fly into ATL/BHM, drive doable. Meals covered minimize extras. Smart budget for big suffering.

biased_opinion.summary: Cheaha doesn't mess around—126 miles stacking 13,000ft over Alabama's roof will humble even hardened hammers. UCI qualifier prestige meets backwoods brutality in a package that's grown from campus century to national beast. Double Cheaha summits and cheeky climb names like 'OH SHIFT!' deliver suffering with a grin. Family cruise sideshows and post-ride feasts make it a ritual worth the pilgrimage. If you crave road pain with heart, this is your crucible.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Elite Southern sufferfest for vert addicts. UCI dreams meet mountain mayhem. Pin this if climbs are your kink.

final_verdict.one_liner: Alabama's vertical vice-grip crushes with prestige and pain.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're vert-hungry with UCI qualifier ambitions or crave legendary Southern climbs. Skip if flats are your jam or cutoffs scare you—stick to rails-trails.
final_verdict.alternatives: Gran Fondo New York for urban prestige, Hincapie Gran Fondo for Southeast vibes, Haute Route for pure vert overload.

logistics.airport: ATL (2hr drive) or BHM (1.5hr) for easiest access. Smaller regional fields work too. Car essential for Alabama backroads.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Crash on JSU campus or Jacksonville square inns. Book early for festival weekend. Camp nearby for immersion.
logistics.food: Pre/post-ride meals covered—BBQ heaven awaits. Award-winning 'cue spots dot the square. Expo fuels the fest.
logistics.packet_pickup: BikeExpo at Pete Mathews Coliseum—grab and gab.

history.origin_story: Born as a humble century over Alabama's highest peak, Mount Cheaha, starting from Jacksonville State University campus. NEABA crafted a loop of pain that hooked riders instantly. Evolved into the state's biggest bike bash, adding Ultra brutality and UCI glory. Now 31 years strong, it's the South's toughest tradition.
notable_moments: First USA UCI Gran Fondo qualifier
Grew to riders from 26 states/9 countries
history.reputation: Toughest ride in the South with heart. Legendary for climbs that break and bond. UCI stamp cements elite status amid family charm.

course_description.character: Back-and-forth paved punishment through Talladega National Forest, doubling down on Alabama's iconic summits and gaps. Hilly hellscape mixes grinding ascents with white-knuckle descents. Ultra extends the agony with remote lake loops—pure, relentless road savagery.

suffering_zones.label: Cheaha Mountain Summit #1
suffering_zones.desc: First taste of Alabama's roof at 2,407ft—3-mile KOM grinder to Bunker Tower. Legs scream amid state park vistas. Turning point looms far away.

suffering_zones.label: Cheaha Mountain Summit #2
suffering_zones.desc: Second helping hits harder post-turnaround. Cumulative fatigue turns pitch to pitchfork. Iconic suffering etched in legend.

suffering_zones.label: Adam’s Gap Turnaround
suffering_zones.desc: Double-dose gap climb marks the pivot—pure mental warfare. Ultra riders press on to Chinnabee cruelty. No mercy in sight.

suffering_zones.label: OH SHIFT! Kicker
suffering_zones.desc: Sneaky named punch after early rollers. Sets the tone for endless ups. Grins fade fast here.

course_description.signature_challenge: Double ascent of Mount Cheaha—Alabama's highest at 2,407ft—bookending the century loop at miles 42 and 57. Each 3-mile haul to Bunker Tower demands everything, turning fit riders to wreckage. The Ultra's extra miles make it a vert odyssey for the damned.

guide_variables.race_terrain: Paved mountainous loops with brutal climbs and descents
guide_variables.race_weather: Mild 60-80F spring with blue skies and humidity risks

non_negotiables.why: Ultra demands vert tolerance—Cheaha doubles will destroy unprepared legs. Build base to survive cutoffs. Test doubles early or reroute to shame.
White-knuckle drops follow every summit. Practice speed to save seconds. Hesitation costs cutoffs in this beast.

citations.title: Cheaha Challenge Granfondo - UCI Gran Fondo World Series
